behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification judgment on explicit imagery. A stubbed classifier can only confirm routing/flag handling and would miss misclassification failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a moderation test with real explicit-image fixtures against the real vision model path; assert each submission is flagged and lands in held-for-review state (not published).

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model robustness to obfuscation variants. Deterministic mocks hide the exact failure this behavior targets: model failing to detect transformed hate speech.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech text variants through the real text moderation model and assert all are flagged/held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a module-handoff/orchestration risk (results from both checks into publish decision and persistence). It does not require real OS/network behavior, and the behavior text does not require judging model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, decision logic, queue/store, and publisher in-process; feed deterministic "pass" outcomes for image and text checks; assert final state is published and no dropped record.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher levels rejected because behavior text focuses on publish-state handoff after passing checks, not real model-quality evaluation or real network/OS failure modes.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: The requirement spans multiple agentic steps in sequence (vision + text model calls) plus orchestration latency. A lower-level test can miss compounded latency and sequencing effects across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through real image and text moderation calls and decision path; measure submit-to-final-decision latency and assert p95 (or required threshold) is <= 5s under representative peak load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is dependency unavailability handling (timeouts/connection errors/5xx) and retry enqueue behavior under real infrastructure conditions. Pure mocks can hide transport and retry-trigger edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real HTTP dependency boundary (or controlled failing endpoint) that simulates vision-service downtime; submit image posts and assert they are queued for retry and never auto-approved.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected because correctness here is failover/retry behavior during outage, not model output quality.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: The behavior targets consistency of real model judgment on borderline content across repeated inferences. Stubbed outputs would always appear consistent and cannot expose model variability-related failures.
behaviors.test_description: Repeatedly submit the same borderline-image fixtures through the real vision moderation path within the target window; assert decision consistency rate meets policy threshold and investigate drift/outlier cases.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow rejected: this is repeated single-step model judgment consistency, not a multi-agentic pipeline with distinct model decisions chained per run.
